final_rules.instructions: Severity: CRITICAL. Action: BLOCK. Deploy globally on all request paths. Double URL_DECODE_UNI at priorities 0-1 resolves %2527 to ' and handles fullwidth percent-encoded sequences (e.g., ％２５). Subsequent transforms strip HTML entities, CSS/JS escapes, comments, and whitespace, then lowercase before handing off to the SQLi engine. Covers payloads: %2527+-- chains, mixed-case ADMIN variants, Unicode fullwidth digits/letters (ｉｎ, ａｄｍｉｎ), homoglyphs (а, І), Mongolian/Ideographic whitespace (\u202f, \u205f), and double-percent-encoded OR/AND injections.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Kept as primary rule unchanged. Two URL_DECODE_UNI passes are intentional and necessary for the %2527 double-encoding bypass. REMOVE_NULLS added as final step to handle null-byte injection. This single rule provides the broadest SQLi coverage for the majority of the supplied payloads.

final_rules.instructions: Severity: CRITICAL. Action: BLOCK. Deploy globally alongside the QueryString rule. Inspects each parsed query argument value individually, which is more precise than raw QueryString inspection when attacks are embedded in a specific parameter value. Catches the same payload classes as the QueryString rule but with per-parameter granularity, reducing the risk of a long benign query string masking an attack in one parameter. Complements rather than replaces the QueryString variant because some WAF engines handle raw vs. parsed field matching differently.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Kept as written. AllQueryArguments provides per-parameter value inspection, which is complementary and not redundant with the QueryString field match. No transform changes needed; identical chain ensures consistent normalization.

final_rules.instructions: Severity: HIGH. Action: BLOCK on authentication and account endpoints; CHALLENGE elsewhere. Anomaly/heuristic guardrail that catches: (1) nested %25-encoded quote/space/plus sequences (%2527, %252b, %2520) that survive a single decode pass; (2) runs of four or more percent-encoded bytes indicating aggressive encoding obfuscation; (3) OR/AND keywords followed by Unicode whitespace variants (NARROW NO-BREAK SPACE \u202f, MEDIUM MATHEMATICAL SPACE \u205f) that SQLi engines normalizing only ASCII spaces may miss. Lower WCU cost than SQLi statements; use as a fast pre-filter.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Replaced the original broad nested-encoding regex with a more precise three-branch pattern. Removed the trailing comment anchor branch (--|/*...*/) into a separate dedicated rule. Added Unicode whitespace class coverage for \u202f/\u205f to specifically address payload variants 1, 3, 5. Limited transforms to URL_DECODE + LOWERCASE to keep WCU low and avoid double-decode artifacts in regex context.

final_rules.instructions: Severity: MEDIUM. Action: BLOCK on /login, /auth, /admin, /account paths via scope-down statement; LOG+CHALLENGE on all other paths. Detects trailing SQL comment terminators (--,  #, /* ) after full URL normalization, including after Unicode whitespace. The scope-down statement should be: {"ByteMatchStatement":{"SearchString":"/login","FieldToMatch":{"UriPath":{}},"PositionalConstraint":"CONTAINS","TextTransformations":[{"Priority":0,"Type":"LOWERCASE"}]}}. Minimizes false positives on endpoints where -- can appear legitimately (e.g., CSS in query params, markdown).
final_rules.refinement_notes: Extended the trailing comment regex to include # (MySQL comment) and added Unicode whitespace class [\s\u00a0\u202f\u205f]* before end anchor to catch payloads 1, 3, 5 where NARROW NO-BREAK SPACE or MEDIUM MATHEMATICAL SPACE follows --. Retained double URL_DECODE_UNI to handle %252d%252d double-encoded dashes. Removed the overly broad [[:space:]] POSIX class which is not guaranteed in all AWS WAF regex engines; replaced with explicit Unicode codepoints.

generated_rules.instructions: Severity: CRITICAL. Action: BLOCK. Primary SQLi detector for query string after layered canonicalization. Covers multi-URL-encoded variants like %2527, %252520, comment-obfuscated probes, mixed case, and whitespace tricks.
Severity: CRITICAL. Action: BLOCK. Same layered SQLi inspection across all query parameter values. Recommended because several samples append random parameters while keeping the attack in a value.

Severity: HIGH. Action: BLOCK. Targeted regex for the dominant bypass family: admin plus quote plus optional encoded whitespace/operators plus SQL comment tail. Intended to catch admin' --, admin%2527%2520--, admin%2527%252b--, and similar. Low false-positive profile because it requires SQL metacharacter progression.
Severity: HIGH. Action: BLOCK. Detects tautology probes such as 1or1=1 and spaced/comment-injected variants after repeated decoding. Useful for Cluster 2 samples that normalize into 1 or 1=1.

Severity: MEDIUM. Action: CHALLENGE or BLOCK on high-risk endpoints. Anomaly rule for excessive nested URL encoding patterns common in these bypasses, especially %2527 / %252520 / %252b chains. Use as a secondary guardrail where legitimate double-encoding is rare.

Severity: MEDIUM. Action: LOG or CHALLENGE by default; BLOCK on authentication endpoints. Catches trailing SQL comment terminators after normalization. Best used in combination with login/account request scoping to minimize false positives.
Severity: HIGH. Action: BLOCK. Same admin-quote-comment family for request bodies to cover form-encoded login submissions rather than only query strings.
Severity: HIGH. Action: BLOCK. Body-scoped tautology detector for login forms and POST content. Helps catch normalized versions of the 1or1=1 bypass family.
